extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won the sprint in his home country by jumping wheel-to-wheel through gaps in the final 500m after his Quick-Step leadout plan completely failed.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step's contract uncertainty (all riders unsigned until Tour ends) creates measurable performance pressure that Kittel explicitly mentioned motivating him.
Katusha pushed pace excessively 30km out while leading chase, causing crash in corner that took down race favorites positioned correctly behind working teams.
FINNEY Taylor's 200km solo breakaway for KOM points reveals DNA-level preference for breaks over peloton riding despite massive energy cost.
Team Sky kept THOMAS Geraint near front to protect yellow jersey while FROOME correctly positioned 8 riders back still crashed from chain reaction.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Won despite leadout failure by improvising wheel-to-wheel jumps through gaps, showing adaptability beyond pure leadout dependency.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Fifth place impressive after crashing at 50km/h earlier in stage, showing recovery ability under duress.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Looked positioned to win 5 seconds before finish but faded to tenth, suggesting sprint timing issues or form deficit.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Positioned correctly 8 riders behind working team yet crashed anyway, highlighting unavoidable risk even with perfect tactics.
rider_signals.FINNEY Taylor: positive: Led 99% of 200km stage solo for KOM points despite knowing catch was inevitable, exceptional breakaway commitment.
rider_signals.KUENG Stefan: positive: White jersey retention depends on avoiding work for PORTE, revealing team's internal calculus about protecting his GC position.
